Metadata.title: North Pacific Skies – People and Machines
Metadata.description: Honoring the history of the Aleutian and Kurile campaigns.

Footer.rights: North Pacific Skies Memorial. All Rights Reserved.
Footer.dedication: Dedicated to the veterans of the North Pacific theatre.

Home.title: NORTH PACIFIC SKIES
Home.subtitle: People and Machines
Home.description: A historical archive honoring the American, Japanese, and Soviet forces of the Aleutian and Kurile Campaigns. Discover the men, the aircraft, and the grueling operations in one of the most forgotten theaters of World War II.
Home.card1Desc: Explore the strategic battles of Dutch Harbor, the Aleutian Campaign, and Kurile Operations.
Home.card2Desc: Examine the detailed records of US, Japanese, and Soviet squadrons deployed.
Home.card3Desc: Technical schematics and historical painting schemes, including the PV-1 Venturas.
Home.card4Desc: First-hand accounts, combat reports, and meticulously preserved casualty records.

Campaigns.title: The Aleutian Campaign
Campaigns.description: A forgotten theater characterized by lethal weather, extreme isolation, and the relentless endurance of the men who fought there.

timeline.t1_desc: Japanese forces launch a diversionary strike against the US Naval Base at Dutch Harbor, bringing the war to North American soil and sparking the long Aleutian Campaign.
timeline.t2_desc: US and Canadian forces endure brutal, freezing conditions to establish forward airbases. More aircraft are lost to the sudden, violent storms and zero-visibility fog than to enemy fire.
timeline.t3_desc: Armed with the rugged PV-1 Venturas, the Empire Express squadrons push the offensive from the Aleutians deep into the Japanese-held Kurile Islands, conducting relentless bombing runs.
timeline.t4_desc: Soviet forces execute a massive amphibious assault on the fortified island of Shumshu. The ensuing battle became the final, bloody engagement of the Pacific Theater.

Units.subtitle: UNITED STATES NAVAL AIR UNITS
Units.title: Imperial & Allied Squadrons
Units.description: Detailed historic archives covering the various squadrons deployed during the hostile weather wars of the Aleutian and Kurile campaigns.

Aircraft.title: Lockheed PV-1 Ventura
Aircraft.description: The Lockheed PV-1 Ventura was the rugged workhorse of the North Pacific. Fighting blinding fogs, severe icing, and relentless winds, the Venturas required immense skill and bravery just to keep them in the sky.
